summary: Great soundboard capture of a sloppy yet high‑energy early‑’89 kickoff show, highlighted by a quirky flange/chorus effect on Phil’s bass.
review: The recording delivers solid soundboard quality that lets the energy of this first‑'89 show shine through, even though the first set is described as a bit sloppy. Listeners note an odd flange/chorus on Phil Lesh’s bass during “Stranger,” which adds a non‑Dead texture but doesn’t detract from the overall vibe. It’s a decent snapshot of the band warming up for a three‑night run.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one moderate (3‑star) review; shows average musical performance with some technical quirks but overall good soundboard quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally great soundboard clarity, though a strange flange/chorus effect appears on Phil's bass during "Stranger"—likely an FX routing mishap.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as sloppy but energetic, serving as a warm‑up for the upcoming three‑night run; overall vibe remains positive despite minor performance roughness.

band_member_comments.Phil: Bass has unexpected flange/chorus on "Stranger," noted as a non‑Dead sounding effect.
